blockingIssues.description: `types_compatible` now treats every `TParam` as compatible with every type in both directions. That means generic type parameters stop enforcing any constraint at all: e.g. the existing negative tests `invalid_generic_return` and `invalid_generic_assign` in `test/test_typecheck.ml` rely on `TParam` not matching arbitrary concrete types, but under this rule `TParam "T"` will accept `i32`, `str`, etc. The requested fix was to preserve concrete generic arguments during `Self` substitution, not to make generic parameters universal wildcards.

The new struct/enum compatibility rule accepts nominal generic types with matching names whenever either side has an empty argument list. That makes bare `Box` compatible with `Box<i32>`, `Box<str>`, etc. everywhere, not just in the specific bare-literal/associated-function `Self` substitution path this feature was meant to repair. Existing negative coverage such as `invalid_bare_vs_instantiated` in `test/test_typecheck.ml` shows this distinction matters.

In commit `8ee29fc`, `check_struct_variant_literal` constructed `TEnum (type_name.node, [])` for every struct variant literal and returned it directly, so expressions like `[Self::Some { value }]` inside `impl Box<T>` lost `T` and typed as bare `Box` instead of `Box<T>`. That breaks valid generic enum struct variant construction and means the broader implementation did not safely satisfy the requested parser fix until the later follow-up feature `misc-fix-generic-enum-struct-variant-type-args` corrected it.

orchestratorOverride.reason: Original misc-followups-3 scrutiny failures were superseded by follow-up milestone misc-followups-3-fix. The fixes were implemented in features misc-fix-generic-type-compatibility-soundness and misc-fix-generic-enum-struct-variant-type-args, and that follow-up milestone passed both scrutiny and user-testing.
